_**User (2026-07-13 09:53:15Z)**_

what are vs code system builtin instructions 

---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-haiku-4.5)**_

The user is asking about VS Code system builtin instructions. This is a general question about VS Code's features and capabilities.

VS Code has several built-in instruction/documentation systems:

1. **Command Palette** - Access via Ctrl+Shift+P (or Cmd+Shift+P on Mac), shows all available commands
2. **Quick Open** - Ctrl+P to quickly open files
3. **Integrated Help** - F1 opens command palette with help
4. **IntelliSense** - Provides code completion and documentation
5. **Hover Information** - Hover over code to see documentation/type information
6. **Go to Definition** - F12 or Ctrl+Click to navigate to definitions
7. **Documentation in UI** - Built-in welcome page, settings UI, extensions marketplace
8. **Keyboard Shortcuts** - Ctrl+K Ctrl+S to view all keyboard shortcuts
9. **Settings UI** - Ctrl+, to open settings with search and documentation
10. **Extensions Documentation** - Each extension has help/documentation
